title: Community Page - Website Metric
description:
(sent every monday)

 
Hello, 

I am checking in to follow through on a project we previously worked on. 
Along with the set up of the Community Page on the website, we had discussed sharing key metrics on a weekly basis in summary. 

I am hoping to set up that system. 
Key Metrics I am looking for: 
  • website clicks (general entry to the website) 
  • website clicks on the community page 
  • how long people stay on the community page/its blog posts 
  • how many people booked jobs (also what time of day if possible) 
I would like a daily breakdown of these metrics, shared weekly if possible. Please let me know if you have access to any other metrics that might be helpful for me to track.
Ideally, I would like to have these metrics presented via email. 

Please let me know what you are able to do regarding this request.

title: Update guarantees page
description:
Please update the contents for their guarantees page.
https://reimerhvac.com/guarantees/

Here are the contents to use: 
Our Guarantees

Peace of Mind

• All our technicians are background checked, drug tested, and receive ongoing training for your peace of mind and expert service. 
Installation Date

• If we do not have your system or generator installed and operating by the date promised, we will pay you $500 for your inconvenience. 
Protection 

• During our visit to your home, your property will be well protected. In the unlikely event that any of your belongings are damaged, we will ensure they are repaired or replaced to your satisfaction. 
No Frustration 

• If your hot water tank or tankless system breaks down during the first year and leave you without hot water, we will be there in 24 hours, or we will pay you $500 for your inconvenience. 
Comfort 

• We ensure that the HVAC systems we install in your home will heat or cool as promised. If the temperature range we guaranteed is not met, we will repair or replace the system at no cost to you. 
Money Back

• After investing in one of our recommended home comfort systems, if you are not 100% satisfied within the first year, we will do everything in our power to make things right, or refund you 100% of your money. 
No Surprise

• At Reimer, we believe in transparent pricing. The quote you receive upfront is exactly what you'll pay, no hidden fees or unexpected charged. You can have peace of mind knowing that any additional work will only proceed with your written approval. 
• Please note that permit fees are not included in the initial estimate and will be billed separately, as determined by local municipalities. 
No Lemon 

• If the compressor, the core of a new air conditioning system, fails twice within the first five years if installation, we will replace your air conditioner and provide you with a brand new warranty at no charge. 
• If the heat exchanger in a new furnace fails within the first ten years, we will replace your furnace and offer a new warranty at no additional cost. 
• If a generator engine fails twice in the first five years, we will install a complete and new generator. 
• If the transfer switch failed twice in the first three years, we will install a new transfer switch. 
• If the tank liner or heat exchanger, the heart of your new water heating system, fails twice within the first five years after installation and the system has been properly maintained, we will replace the entire unit at no cost and provide you with a brand new warranty.

Thanks much!

title: Community Page - Website Metric
description:
(sent every monday)

 
Hello, 

I am checking in to follow through on a project we previously worked on. 
Along with the set up of the Community Page on the website, we had discussed sharing key metrics on a weekly basis in summary. 

I am hoping to set up that system. 
Key Metrics I am looking for: 
  • website clicks (general entry to the website) 
  • website clicks on the community page 
  • how long people stay on the community page/its blog posts 
  • how many people booked jobs (also what time of day if possible) 
I would like a daily breakdown of these metrics, shared weekly if possible. Please let me know if you have access to any other metrics that might be helpful for me to track.
Ideally, I would like to have these metrics presented via email. 

Please let me know what you are able to do regarding this request.

title: Create a Reimer Water Filtration Page
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Predrag, Venus Saragena
description:
Seeking help with the client request below:

I wanted to request a webpage build to include in our plumbing services portion. We’re working with this brand to push water filtration installs and would like to create a landing page for materials we’re putting together. The page should feature information on the Halo systems providing a brief description of how it works and the benefits of safe, clean water.
 
Please also include a paragraph to book your Plumbing Maintenance and Safety Inspection with Reimer to test your home’s water levels for harmful contaminants. Just because it’s legal doesn’t always mean it’s safe. (https://www.ewg.org/tapwater/)
 
I’ve included a picture of the Halo product.
We’d also like to feature this informational video on the page to give customers more information.
https://youtu.be/xxBklGLdyAs

Please let me know if you need further info from the client. 

Also may I have an ETA that I can send to them? 

Thanks much!

title: Careers Page
description:
Original client request from Christopher Lanza (Introduced by Kaysen Bickel) April 25

Hi Justyna - nice to meet you and thank you for the quick response! 

We were looking at the careers page and noticed that the current openings only list 3 jobs, HVAC Service Technician, Plumber and HVAC Installer.



When you click on Apply Now, it takes you to the portal where all our current jobs live - around 14 right now. 

I was wondering if we could advertise something more general about our openings to encourage job seekers to click in to see what's available. Having the list of the three trades seems confusing, as if we're only hiring for 3 roles right now. Ultimately whatever is the quickest/easiest way to get people to the Icims landing page to view the job openings (with the least amount of clicks) is what I'd love to see. 
 
Let me know your thoughts and if you have any questions. Thanks!

title: Organic Search Decline Month on Month
description:
Hey I noticed a decline in organic search traffic on this site and did a bit of digging. Here are the findings.

Decline in Organic Clicks and Impressions:
On GSC, I saw a decline in both clicks and impressions month-to-date, and noticed that most of the affected pages were brand-related, so the homepage and location pages were the main culprits:



Here are the main pages that saw a decline in clicks:

Branded Search Decline:
A branded search decline normally means the site is affected by a manual action or penalty, and paid campaigns have been restructured, or there are competitors bidding on their brand. It looks like it is related to paid campaigns, as they have Google Ads running.

When doing spot checks for branded searches using the same keywords that have declined within their location, the first clickable result is not the brand most of the time. Here are some of the results:

Paid Location pack Result:



On mobile, we have what looks like directories that are appearing before the brand as well:


Conclusion:
The decline is definitely not an SEO related issue, but it is something we can flag with the client. We could possibly request having their Google Ads account linked to GA4, so we can have a look at some campaigns and we can monitor performance results that harm overall SEO performance. This is not to blame their paid search partners, but to ensure that the overall performance of the site is prioritized.

I would recommend sending this info to the client. Looping in for final approval as we may need a few more spot checks before confirming that indeed there's brand bidding or paid campaign issues at play.

title: GSC: Crawled Not Indexed URLs
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Evgenii Gusarovskii, Joel Nkadimeng, Jordan, Predrag
description:
can we please assign this task for implementation. We seemed to have had a URL structure update where we had two versions of blogs, one using a root URL (currently live on the site) and the correct /blog/ structure. Google decided to crawl and not index both versions, even though there are redirects in place. We have blogs that look like they're commercial pages, like this one:  https://reimerhvac.com/leaking-drains-solutions 

Here is the link: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1ceVY0CwL0E4K2NpLeig2uBeQEeAs--zaEbVMNgLls6o/edit?usp=sharing

This might need developers because we need to:
  • Reverse the change from /blog/ to root slug structure or assign parent folders
  • Have these linked to the blog section
  • ensure all canonicals point to /blog/ versions
  • Update the XML sitemap

title: Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites
description:
Hi, this is inline with previous task - Include Sitemap In footer of Client websites - J389 Go Arco

Here is a sheet we can also use for reference: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1qPfUUC1J2r1s7Ot8Y1PmIcje6WpyMszDhxHGz4TxDTk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

SEO added more info column D. 

I have included the task info here:
--- 
Hey Team!

To enhance our technical SEO baseline, improve crawl efficiency, and ensure consistent signaling across all client properties, It would be great to come to an agreement to outlining a new, standardized protocol for crawl management.

My suggestion is as follows:

Implement the following three directives on all client websites as part of our standard build and maintenance process.

1. XML Sitemap in robots.txt (Primary Directive)

This is the most effective way to inform search engines of all indexable URLs.

Action: Ensure the robots.txt file for every client domain contains a Sitemap directive pointing to the full URL of the XML sitemap.

Implementation Example:
User-agent: 
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap.xml

The "Why": This provides a clear, direct path for Google to find and crawl our most important pages, ensuring faster discovery and indexing of new content.

2. User-Facing HTML Sitemap (Footer)

This is for user experience and secondary crawl pathing.

Action: All client websites must have a user-facing HTML sitemap.

Implementation: This sitemap must be linked in the footer of every page on the site.

The "Why": This provides a clear navigation path for users, improves site accessibility, and   acts as a secondary crawl path for search engines to discover deep pages.

Strategic Crawl & Indexation Control

This is critical. We must be precise in telling crawlers what to crawl and what to index.

A. To Control Crawling (Using robots.txt)

We must Disallow crawlers from accessing low-value, non-SEO pages to conserve crawl budget.

Action: Audit and update the robots.txt file to Disallow non-public or utility sections of the site.
Implementation Examples:
Disallow: /wp-admin/

The WP sitemap page  plugin in perfect to use as a plugin for now

Regarding providing a robot.txt:

They differ per client especially regarding disallows but here is a comprehensive one that can be edited according to client needs especially regarding disallows:

User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.clientdomain.com/sitemap_index.xml

Also, just an example of placement. Sitemap listed at the very bottom of the footer on the left followed by privacy policy etc.

* In the footer we will use the page /sitemap/ and not the XML sitemap.  The XML sitemap is for Google & the robots.txt.


you can use this as robots.txt:
 
User-agent: *

# --- Core WordPress File Disallows ---
# Block all core administrative and include folders.
Disallow: /wp-admin/
Disallow: /wp-includes/

# --- Crawl Budget Optimization ---
# Block non-media plugin and theme files from being crawled.
Disallow: /wp-content/plugins/
Disallow: /wp-content/themes/

# Block internal search result pages, which are low-quality, duplicate content.
Disallow: /?s=
Disallow: /search/

# Block other non-public WordPress files and pages.
Disallow: /wp-login.php
Disallow: /readme.html
Disallow: /trackback/
Disallow: /xmlrpc.php

# --- Allow Directives for Critical Files ---
# Explicitly ALLOW critical AJAX and asset files that Google 
# may need for rendering, even if their parent folder is disallowed.
Allow: /wp-admin/admin-ajax.php
Allow: /wp-content/uploads/

# --- Sitemap Directive ---
# This is the primary directive to tell all search engines where our XML sitemap is located.
# [IMPORTANT: Replace with the client's actual sitemap URL]
Sitemap: https://www.goarco.com/sitemap_index.xml

Thanks much!

title: Two Issues
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag, Venus Saragena
description:
Issue 1. JSON error prevents the saving of changes to Meta Titles and Meta Descriptions on pages.
Note: You can make any changes to those sections, save the page, but if you close the page and re-open it, you will notice that the changes were reverted.


I have found this error on the pages published in January 2025:

https://reimerhvac.com/rochester/electrical/panel-upgrade/
https://reimerhvac.com/rochester/electrical/ev-charger-installation/
https://reimerhvac.com/rochester/electrical/usb-outlet-installation/
https://reimerhvac.com/rochester/electrical/new-appliance-wiring/
https://reimerhvac.com/rochester/electrical/basement-wiring/
https://reimerhvac.com/rochester/electrical/rewiring/

However, it probably applies to all pages. The error does not appear in posts.

Issue 2. Please make the contextual links visible in Wordpress. This isn't exactly a super issue, but will considerably improve our work when we have to interlink pages or add external links.

title: Add Content (Rochester Location Page Additional Content)
description:
• https://reimerhvac.com/rochester/
           Change the text from Cooling, Heating, and Plumbing Services in Rochester, NY to Cooling, Heating, Plumbing, and Electrical Services in Rochester, NY
  • Add this content to the page.

     

    Electrical Services in Rochester, NY


    At Reimer, our electrical services are comprehensive, covering installations, upgrades, and maintenance to meet all your electrical needs. Our team of skilled electricians provides expert electrical wiring and rewiring services, ensuring safety and functionality in both residential and commercial settings.


    We specialize in the installation of electrical outlets, lighting systems, and electrical panels, balancing aesthetic appeal with practical requirements. For electric vehicle owners, our EV charger installation service offers a convenient solution for home charging.


    Safety is paramount in our services. We provide smoke detector installations, thorough electrical safety inspections, and surge protection to keep your home secure. Additionally, our generator services guarantee that you remain powered during outages, ensuring peace of mind.


    Here is a list of our electrical services:


    • Electrical Wiring & Rewiring:       Essential services for installing new wiring or updating existing systems to enhance safety and efficiency in your home.


    • Electrical Outlet Installation:       Professional installation of electrical outlets, ensuring safe and convenient access to electricity throughout your space.


    • Lighting Installation:       Expert installation of various lighting fixtures to improve ambiance and functionality in your home or business.


    • Electrical Panel Upgrade:       Upgrading electrical panels to accommodate increased power demands and enhance safety standards.


    • Electric Vehicle (EV) Charger Installation:       Seamless installation of EV chargers for convenient home charging of electric vehicles.


    • Circuit Breaker Installation and Repair:       Services to install new or repair existing circuit breakers, ensuring your electrical system remains safe and functional.


    • Smoke Detector Installation:       Installation of smoke detectors to enhance home safety and provide early warnings in case of fire.


    • Electrical Safety Inspections:       Comprehensive inspections to ensure your electrical systems meet safety standards and operate efficiently.


    • Surge Protection:       Installation of surge protection devices to safeguard your home’s appliances from damaging voltage spikes.


    • Generator Installation and Maintenance:       Installation and maintenance of generators, providing a reliable backup power source during outages.


  • We pride ourselves on delivering quality workmanship and exceptional customer service. Whether you’re looking to upgrade your home’s electrical system or require emergency repairs, our team is ready to assist you. Contact us today to learn more about our services or to schedule a consultation!

title: TCPA policy on all Apex Sites & landing pages
assignees: Bogdan Zakharchyshyn, Den, Jordan, Keith Bruss, Predrag, Venus Saragena
description:
Hi, please see request from Debra: 

At Apex, we are looking to make sure all of our websites and landing pages have TCPA policies addressed with user opt in/out. 
 
The key aspect being addressed in this round are forms. This includes both site, landing pages, about us pages, etc. We need to work together to have a complete audit of where we need to add an option for using to consent to receiving marketing materials.
 
 This is the language (where Company Name is the respective Company’s full name):
 
 "By checking this box, I consent to receive marketing and promotional texts, calls, and emails from or on behalf of {Company Name} and its affiliates using an automated system or auto dialer for any purpose, including HVAC, plumbing, and electrical products and services. Consent is not a condition of purchase, and I can opt-out at any time."
 
It's also important to call out that not every business offers HVAC, Plumbing, or Electrical services, so that section also needs to be adjusted accordingly to the respective business. 
--- 
reimerhvac.com services: ac, heating, plumbing, electrical

title: Send Welcome Email
list_title: ONBOARDING › Pre-Discovery ADMIN / ACCOUNT MANAGER / PM
description:
Dear XXXXX (Client Contact),

On behalf of the entire Clixsy staff, I’d like to take this opportunity to welcome you as a new client. We are thrilled to have you with us.

I am also happy to say that I will be your primary point of contact, and please get in touch at any time with your questions, comments and feedback.

NEW CLIENT INTAKE FORM
 
I have linked you to our New Client Intake Form, below. Can you, or a designated individual, please fill it in and submit at your earliest convenience?
 
This New Client Intake Form forms the basis for our Discovery and initial kick-off meeting in which we’ll get together to begin working together and establishing roles, responsibilities and expectations. 
 
Please ensure you thoroughly fill in what you can, and if you are not able to fill something in, just let us know and we’ll work with you to either obtain the information or determine whether it’s a priority at this time. 
 
There are sections with requests for technical information like usernames and passwords for your online assets. Please try to be as accurate as possible with these so we can get in and audit your website/social/advertising accounts without having to return to you with further requests. This may slow the process up and cost us valuable time.
 
 Don’t worry if you can’t, or are unwilling to disclose all the information we have requested as the main goal here is to have at hand any resources we’ll need to work efficiently and to ensure that we organize and maintain current/accurate information for you so that you’ll never have to say “I don’t know” when a vendor or internal partner/employee asks “who has the login to XYZ”. Not to mention any information you can supply will help get the best returns we can from your investment with us. Just try and be as accurate and as thorough as you can. We will cover everything in more depth at your Discovery Meeting. 
 
http://www.clixsy.com/intake/

DISCOVERY MEETING
 
As soon as we have received and processed your New Client Intake Form, I will reach out to find a time to hold your Discovery Meeting. This will be held online via a Zoom call. 

 In the Discovery Meeting we will answer any questions you may have and talk about who on our team addresses the various roles and responsibilities, what next steps will be and establish our “battle rhythm” as our teams determine how best to work in unison. We will use the New Client Intake Form to discuss things like, your business and campaign goals, KPIs, customer journeys through your digital assets, your competitors etc. We will brainstorm the best methods to reach your goals and agree a ‘Definition of DONE’, for each stage of the process. 
 
We will also discuss any legal advertising constraints there may be in your industry and set boundaries based on what your company culture is comfortable with, and what is appropriate in the market. 
 
This should give us all the information we need to get started our work in increasing your market share and reaching your business goals. 
 
Once again, thank you for entrusting Clixsy with your business. Together we'll do our best to get you as big an ROI and slice of the market share as we can, as quickly as possible,
 
 
 Best regards,
 
 
 
 R. Corey Vandenberg 
President 
Corey@clixsy.com 
801-645-6466

title: Email Discovery Meeting Agenda to client
Prepare Discovery Meeting Agenda
description:
DISCOVERY MEETING INTERNAL CHECKLIST


Have you discussed the Client's Business/Campaign Goals? One goal per campaign.

Have you built a Typical Customer Journey for the Client's business?

Have you built a Typical Buyer Persona for each campaign?

Have you decided on Campaign KPIs to measure our success?

Have you agreed on Monthly KPI Targets?

Have you set Campaign Budgets?

Have you spoken about common objections to sales the Client has experienced?

Have you done a SWOT analysis on each of the Client's Top 3 competitors?

Has Client intimated any legal constraints to advertising?

Have you discussed Integrated Marketing and looked at Client's past and current advertising campaigns?

Has the Client brought images/logos etc.?

Have you brainstormed ideas for creatives/messages/slogans/copy etc.?

Have you suggested any further services that could be beneficial to the Client?

If the CPC has changed have you transferred changes to Final Client Contract (FCC)?

Have you agreed the new Price & Payment Terms?

Have you written up the Final Client Contract (FCC) while Client is still in the office?

Have you got the Final Client Contract (FCC) signed?

Have you discussed incentives/discounts for referrals?

Have you asked for referrals? Add the on the next sheet.
